context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T21:53Z after continuing product/extensibility work. Branch develop is intentionally local-ahead of origin/develop by 7 commits because user requested batching local work instead of pushing/watching GitHub Actions for every slice. Worktree should be clean after this handoff commit. Disk remains constrained but stable at ~13GB free on /workspaces/refarm; no cleanup was necessary this turn.
current_tasks: Latest remote-pushed/CI-green baseline remains 4e02e548 `refactor(homestead): scope surface context providers` unless pushed later.

Local commits ahead of origin/develop: 1f980843 `feat(app-dev): prove registry validated surfaces`; 6c54c16c `chore(backlog): note e2e pipeline speedups`; 109bd170 `feat(homestead): share plugin registry helper`; e5ce03ee `chore(project): checkpoint registry surface flow`; 8c2ec2ac `feat(homestead): dispatch surface host actions`; dfd2b65f `refactor(homestead): scope surface action handlers`; 5e77390b `docs(homestead): clarify studio primitive boundary`.

1f980843: apps/dev `/surfaces` diagnostics now include external `validated` surface fixture (`external-validated-surface`) that registers with Tractor registry status `validated` before shell setup, proving the real Homestead registry trust gate in addition to internal trusted, failing render, and untrusted external fixtures.

109bd170: Homestead SDK gained `registerStudioPluginManifest(...)` in `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/plugin-handle`, so hosts can mark local/resolved plugin manifests as `validated`/`active` without hiding explicit plugin handle registration.

8c2ec2ac: Homestead SDK gained generic host-owned surface action dispatch. `StudioShellOptions.surfaceAction` accepts `HomesteadSurfaceRenderActionHandler`; rendered controls opt in via `data-refarm-surface-action-id`; `StudioShell` resolves actions from host context, emits `ui:surface_action_requested` and `ui:surface_action_failed`, and delegates concrete effects to the host. apps/dev implements only its concrete `studio:navigate` demo behavior.

dfd2b65f: Homestead SDK gained `createScopedHomesteadSurfaceActionHandler(...)`, mirroring `createScopedHomesteadSurfaceContextProvider(...)`, so reusable matching is SDK-owned while host semantics stay app-owned. apps/dev stream demo now uses this helper for the stream surface action handler.
5e77390b: docs clarify terminology: Studio is a shared Homestead primitive for all first-party apps; `apps/dev` is the reference workbench, not the owner of reusable mechanics.

Local validations passed after latest code slices: `npm --prefix packages/homestead test -- Shell.test.ts surface-renderer.test.ts`; `npm --prefix packages/homestead test -- surface-renderer.test.ts`; `npm --prefix packages/homestead run type-check`; `npm --prefix packages/homestead run build`; `npm --prefix apps/dev test -- stream-demo.test.ts`; `npm --prefix apps/dev run type-check`; `npm --prefix apps/dev run build`; `npm run project:validate --silent`; `git diff --check`.

During apps/dev test after adding scoped action helper, the first run failed because apps/dev imported `@refarm.dev/homestead` from dist and Homestead had not been rebuilt. Running `npm --prefix packages/homestead run build` fixed it. Remember to build Homestead before app tests when SDK exports change.
Known Vite browser-externalization warnings appeared during apps/dev build for `node:fs/promises` and `node:path`; unchanged/non-blocking. No sqlite benchmark worker diff appeared after builds.
blockers: Do not push/watch GitHub Actions after every small slice. User preference remains: validate locally, accumulate meaningful package, then push/monitor CI once.
Disk is stable at ~13GB free but host remains 98% used. No cleanup was run. Use `npm run clean:light --silent` only if generated artifacts grow meaningfully or free space drops near 2-3GB.
CI/E2E speed work is explicitly backlogged in `.pi/todos/e1279bab.md`; do not switch to that lane unless requested or after product/extensibility batch is ready.
Builds can rewrite `validations/sqlite-benchmark/browser/public/sqlite3-worker1.mjs` by inlining the LANG expression. Inspect and restore unless intentional.
Avoid broad Rust rebuilds unless Rust is touched. Follow AGENTS.md resource discipline if Rust becomes necessary.
Project task append tooling previously failed because old `.project/tasks.json` entries have schema-invalid `phase: null`. Avoid appending tasks there unless normalizing old phases intentionally.

Good atomic slice candidate: add semantic Homestead inspector helpers for surface action telemetry (`ui:surface_action_requested`/`ui:surface_action_failed`) and render them in apps/dev statusbar or `/surfaces`, preserving surface-inspector as read-only semantic and presenters in app layer.

Good atomic slice candidate: formalize `renderHomesteadSurface` and `data-refarm-surface-action-id` in plugin manifest docs/examples, showing external validated plugins can render text/actions without app coupling.
